quoteLast: , Custer called the officers together, and      informed us that beyond a doubt the village was in the valley of      the Little Big Horn, and that to reach it, it was necessary to      cross the divide between the Rosebud and Little Big Horn, and it      would be impossible to do so in the daytime without discovering our      march to the Indians; that we would prepare to move at 11 P.M. This      was done, the line of march turning from the Rosebud to the right,      up one of its branches, which headed near the summit of the divide.
title:
